evals.expected_output: Bootstrap helper called first; live macro + ticker prices searched with timestamps; quote_table populated and rendered visibly at top of HTML; 11 mandatory sections present; JSON persisted via write_brief.py with no validation errors; recommendations.json updated; HTML file written.

assertions.name: init_brief_called_first
assertions.description: init_brief.py was invoked before any web search or analysis (visible in tool calls).

assertions.name: live_macro_searched
assertions.description: Web searches performed for SPX/NDX futures, VIX, oil, gold, 10Y, DXY, BTC, ETH with current timestamps (not prior close).

assertions.name: quote_table_nonempty
assertions.description: Step 1 Part E quote table contains every ticker that will be recommended, each with live_price, timestamp, and source.

assertions.name: every_rec_in_quote_table
assertions.description: Every recommendation's ticker appears in the quote_table — Step 4 gate enforced.

assertions.name: step6_citation_per_trade
assertions.description: Each recommendation has a step6_citation field naming the search query or URL and timestamp.

assertions.name: min_seven_dte
assertions.description: Every recommendation has dte_at_entry >= 7, OR short_dte_exception=true with all five exception conditions documented.

assertions.name: all_eleven_sections_rendered
assertions.description: HTML output contains all 11 mandatory sections in order, plus the visible Part E quote table at the top.

assertions.name: carry_forward_section_present
assertions.description: Section 3 (Open Positions Carry-Forward) is rendered. If no open positions, it shows the explicit empty-state line, not a blank section.

assertions.name: json_persisted
assertions.description: write_brief.py was invoked with the brief JSON, returned ok:true, and morning-briefs/YYYY-MM-DD.json + recommendations.json exist on disk.

assertions.name: html_written
assertions.description: morning-briefs/YYYY-MM-DD.html written; standalone HTML; includes all three cdn.jsdelivr.net font imports and @media print styles.

assertions.name: no_memory_priced_tickers
assertions.description: No recommendation has a price_source containing 'memory', 'estimate', 'approximate', or 'last I knew'.

evals.expected_output: Backfill mode: --date 2026-04-23 passed to init_brief.py. The skill must still verify live prices for that date (or refuse with explicit message that historical pricing is unavailable for the brief). Carry-forward set is computed from recommendations.json as of 2026-04-23. Brief written to morning-briefs/2026-04-23.json and .html. The NOW $730 / MSTR $330 fabrication failures from the v3.3 era must NOT recur — the skill either sources live April-23 prices or refuses to recommend strikes for tickers it cannot price.

assertions.name: date_override_used
assertions.description: init_brief.py was called with --date 2026-04-23 and the JSON brief_date field is 2026-04-23.

assertions.name: no_fabricated_strikes
assertions.description: No recommendation uses strikes that are structurally impossible relative to the live price (e.g. NOW $680/$780 strikes for a $90 stock). If live price for the historical date cannot be sourced, the skill refuses to recommend that ticker.

assertions.name: validation_passed
assertions.description: write_brief.py returned ok:true with no validation_warnings.

evals.prompt: Run the morning brief — focus on tech earnings this week.
evals.expected_output: Sector focus is honored: implied_move_watch and high_conviction sections are weighted toward tech earnings names. The carry_forward section, quote_table discipline, and 11 mandatory sections are NOT skipped just because of the focus filter. JSON + HTML produced as normal.

assertions.name: tech_focus_honored
assertions.description: Section 4 and Section 5 are weighted toward tech-sector earnings names without dropping any mandatory section.

assertions.name: all_eleven_sections_still_rendered
assertions.description: Sector focus does not cause any of the 11 mandatory sections to be skipped.

assertions.name: quote_table_complete
assertions.description: Every ticker recommended (including the tech earnings names) appears in quote_table with sourced live price.

evals.expected_output: Verifies the carry-forward path. If recommendations.json contains an open position from a prior date with expiry >= today, that position MUST appear in carry_forward_reviewed with action_today set. The persist script merges that review into the existing recommendation's reviews[] array.

assertions.name: carry_forward_loaded_from_init
assertions.description: init_brief.py output's carry_forward array was used to populate Section 3 — the skill did not re-read recommendations.json itself.

assertions.name: every_open_position_reviewed
assertions.description: Every entry in init_brief.py's carry_forward output appears in the brief's carry_forward_reviewed array with action_today set.

assertions.name: current_price_sourced
assertions.description: Each carry-forward entry has a current_stock_price that came from the quote_table (the ticker also appears in quote_table).

assertions.name: review_merged_into_index
assertions.description: After write_brief.py runs, recommendations.json shows the prior recommendation with a new entry appended to its reviews[] array containing review_date, action_today, current_stock_price, current_pnl_pct.

evals.expected_output: Tests v3.4 failure modes. If any candidate ticker cannot be sourced, the skill MUST drop it from recommendations rather than fabricate. write_brief.py must reject any brief where a recommendation's ticker is not in quote_table, where price_source is missing, or where dte_at_entry < 7 without short_dte_exception.

assertions.name: missing_ticker_rejected
assertions.description: If a test brief is constructed with a recommendation whose ticker is not in quote_table, write_brief.py exits non-zero with a 'Step 4 gate failed' error message.

assertions.name: missing_source_rejected
assertions.description: If a recommendation has empty price_source, write_brief.py exits non-zero with a 'Step 6 #12 gate failed' error message.

assertions.name: short_dte_without_exception_rejected
assertions.description: If a recommendation has dte_at_entry=3 without short_dte_exception=true, write_brief.py exits non-zero.
